person.name: Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles

parts.value: of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
William Hay of Erroll, succeeded to the estates in 1436, and had sasine of the lands of Inchira on 25 January 1443-44, on a precept from Joanna Beaufort, Queen of James I, and to him and Beatrix his wife on 30 January 1449-50, on his own resignation.  He granted a charter of the lands of Achmore to Sir David Hay of Yester in 1452.

According to Douglas, he was created EARL OF ERROLL 17 March 1452-53, but the Auchenleck Chronicle states that he was belted Earl in the Parliament held 12 June 1452.  As Earl of Erroll, he resigned all his lands into the King’s hands to be erected into the lordship of Erroll and regality of Slains respectively, on which resignation he had on 31 july 1452 two charters under the Great Seal, one of the lordship of Errol and the other of the regality of Slains, to himself and a long series of heirs.  It was in virtue of these charters that the title and estates passed to the heir-male in 1541, and remained a male fief till the destination was changed in the time of Charles II.

The Earl was alive on 15 November 1461, so that the date of his death in the Erroll Obits, 24 August 1460, must be wrong.  The true date is probably October 1462.

He married, before 17  March 1449-50, Beatrix Douglas, daughter of James, seventh Earl of Douglas, who survived him.

William, first Earl, had issue:

1.  Nicholas, second Earl
2.  William, third Earl.
3.  Gilbert, a substitute in a charter of 1460 by his father of the lands of Meikle Arnage.  He probably died young.
4.  Elizabeth, who was contracted in marriage …with Sir Andrew Gray …
5.  Margaret, married, first, about 1470 to Alexander Fraser, fourth of Philorth …
6.  Isabel, married Laurence, first Lord Oliphant.

Source:  THE SCOTS PEERAGE, ed. by Sir James Balfour Paul, Vol III, Edinburgh, 1906, pp. 564-5
------------------------------

Father	Gilbert de la Haye d. 7 Sep 1436
Mother	Alice Hay
Charts	Pedigree of James Irvine
     Sir William Hay, 1st Earl of Erroll, 2nd Lord Hay, Constable of Scotland was born at of Erroll, Perthshire, Scotland. He married Beatrix Douglas, daughter of James Douglas, 7th Earl Douglas, 1st Earl Avondale and Beatrix St. Clair, before 17 March 1450.3,6 Sir William Hay, 1st Earl of Erroll, 2nd Lord Hay, Constable of Scotland died in October 1462 at Slains.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles於1412年出生在英國蘇格蘭培爾埃羅爾時，他的父親Gilbert de la Haye17歲，母親Alicia Hay17歲。他於1449年3月17日與Beatrice Douglas在英國蘇格蘭培爾埃羅爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1462年10月8日在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾可利艾斯頓享年50歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭費伏帕爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles于1412年出生在英国苏格兰培爾埃羅爾时，他的父亲Gilbert de la Haye17岁，母亲Alicia Hay17岁。他于1449年3月17日与Beatrice Douglas在英国苏格兰培爾埃羅爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1462年10月8日在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾可利艾斯頓享年50岁，埋葬在苏格兰費伏帕爾。

nameMeanings.name: Hay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: High
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Syed, Arifa, Atta, Faiz, Hamid, Iftekhar, Iskandar, Mohamed, Mohammad, Mohannad.  Vietnamese Mui, Pham, Tran, Vo, Bui, Cao, Chau, Dinh, Duong, Nguyen, Sau, Vo Van.  Jewish Ilan, Ronen, Shlomo.  Chinese Chan, Chiming, Lung, Wai Fun, Yin.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): from a personal name based on Arabic ḥayy ‘alive’. Al-Ḥayy ‘the Living’ is an attribute of Allah.
Jewish: variant of   Hay  .

variants.name: Dehay
meanings: French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the masculine form of Old French haye ‘hedge’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word (see Hay 9).
Altered form (also De Hay) of Dutch De Haij or De Haaij, which is probably of French origin (see above).

variants.name: Haig
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hayman
meanings: English: topographic name probably from Middle English hai(e), hei(e) ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg), denoting a dweller by an enclosure (see Hay 2 ), or else a nickname for a ‘keeper of the enclosure’ (compare Hayward ).
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, high(e) (Old English hēah); see also Hay 3.
English: occupational name for a seller of hay, from Middle English hai, hei (Old English hēg, hīg).

variants.name: Hayes
meanings: Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAodha ‘descendant of Aodh’, a personal name meaning ‘fire’. Compare McCoy . In some cases especially in County Wexford, the surname is of English origin (see below), having been taken to Ireland by the Normans.
English: variant of Hay , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: topographic name from the plural form of Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (see Hay ), sometimes used as a collective noun for a farm, especially in Devon, where it is a frequent minor placename. Compare Hain .

variants.name: Haycock
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Hai + coc; see Hay 4 and compare Cocke .

variants.name: Haylett
meanings: English (Norfolk): from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Hai (see Hay 4), from Hai + -el + -ot.

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles was born in 1412, in Errol, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Gilbert de la Haye, was 17 and his mother, Alicia Hay, was 17. He married Beatrice Douglas on 17 March 1449, in Errol, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 8 October 1462, in Collieston, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, at the age of 50, and was buried in Cupar, Fife, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles est né en 1412, à Errol, Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gilbert de la Haye, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Alicia Hay, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Beatrice Douglas le 17 mars 1449, à Errol, Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 8 octobre 1462, à Collieston, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Cupar, Fife, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles 1412 in Errol, Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gilbert de la Haye 17 und seine Mutter Alicia Hay 17. Er hat am 17. März 1449 in Errol, Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Beatrice Douglas geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 8. Oktober 1462 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Collieston, Aberdeenshire, Scotland und wurde in Cupar, Fife, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles è nato il nel 1412 nel Errol, Perthshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Gilbert de la Haye, aveva 17 anni e il suo madre, Alicia Hay, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Beatrice Douglas il 17 marzo 1449, nel Errol, Perthshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 8 ottobre 1462, nel Collieston, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Cupar, Fife, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peeblesが1412年にイギリススコットランドペルスシュアイアエロルで生まれた時，父Gilbert de la Hayeは17歳，母Alicia Hayは17歳であった。1449年3月17日にイギリススコットランドペルスシュアイアエロルでBeatrice Douglasと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1462年10月8日にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアコリーストンで50歳で死去し，スコットランドフィフクウパーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles(이)가 1412년 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 에럴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert de la Haye(은)는 17세였고, 그의 어머니 Alicia Hay(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1449년 3월 17일 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 에럴에서 Beatrice Douglas(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1462년 10월 8일 스컷랜드 압르디실르 콜리스턴에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 피프 c업아르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles nasceu em 1412, em Errol, Perthshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Gilbert de la Haye, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Alicia Hay, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Beatrice Douglas em 17 de março de 1449, em Errol, Perthshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 8 de outubro de 1462, em Collieston, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Cupar, Fife, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles родился в 1412 г. в местности эррол, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Gilbert de la Haye было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Alicia Hay, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrice Douglas 17 марта 1449 г. в местности эррол, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Он умер 8 октября 1462 г. в местности коллиестон, абирдиншир, Шотландия в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности купар, фиф, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Hay of Locherworth and Yester, Sheriff of Peebles nació en 1412, en Errol, Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Gilbert de la Haye, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Alicia Hay, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Beatrice Douglas el 17 de marzo de 1449, en Errol, Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 8 de octubre de 1462, en Collieston, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, a la edad de 50 años, y fue sepultado en Cupar, Fife, Scotland.
